title: probeWebGpuAdapter() crashes on Node.js: bare navigator access without guard
description: Every other `navigator` access in this file is guarded with `typeof navigator !== 'undefined'`. Line 283 accesses `(navigator as any)?.gpu` directly without that guard. In Node.js (where `navigator` is undefined) this throws `ReferenceError: navigator is not defined` at runtime, making the exported public API unsafe on Node. The function is exported and callers in a Node environment could reasonably call it.

title: Capabilities.libjxlVersion is permanently 'unknown' despite a typed string contract
description: The `Capabilities` interface declares `libjxlVersion: string`, implying a real version is available. The implementation always assigns the literal `'unknown'` with a TODO comment. Any consumer branching on this field (e.g. `if (caps.libjxlVersion.startsWith('0.11'))`) will silently receive `'unknown'` forever. The contract should either be `libjxlVersion: string | null` with `null` meaning unavailable, or the field should be removed/typed as a discriminated union so callers cannot misuse it.
code_excerpt: libjxlVersion: "unknown", // TODO(packages/jxl-wasm/scripts/build.mjs): emit consumable libjxl version const

title: recommendedEffort() return type declares 1–9 but only 4, 6, or 7 are reachable
description: The declared return type `1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5 | 6 | 7 | 8 | 9` implies callers should handle all nine values. In practice only 4, 6, and 7 are ever returned. This misleads consumers who write exhaustive switch statements or numeric range checks, and means the public contract is broader than the implementation. Either narrow the return type to `4 | 6 | 7` or add code paths that produce the missing values.

title: Capabilities.imageDecoder means 'class exists' not 'JXL is supported', conflating two distinct facts
description: `imageDecoder` is set via `typeof (globalThis as any).ImageDecoder !== 'undefined'` — a pure class-existence check. The `probeNativeJxl()` function separately calls `ImageDecoder.isTypeSupported('image/jxl')` to determine JXL support. The field name `imageDecoder` implies JXL-decode capability, but callers checking `caps.imageDecoder` only learn that WebCodecs ImageDecoder exists in the environment, not that JXL decoding works. This is a contract semantic mismatch. `nativeJxlDecoder` already captures the real answer; `imageDecoder` should be named `webCodecsImageDecoder` or its doc comment must clearly state it is only an API presence check.

category: exported API dead code / internal duplication
title: canUseThreadedWasm() is exported but never called internally; detectTier() duplicates its logic inline
description: `detectTier()` (line 105) implements the SAB+COI check inline rather than calling the exported `canUseThreadedWasm(hasSab, crossOriginIsolated)`. If a future caller monkey-patches or overrides `canUseThreadedWasm` in tests, `detectTier` is unaffected and will produce contradictory results. The contract implied by a public export is that the library uses its own API. The fix is for `detectTier` to call `canUseThreadedWasm(hasSab, crossOriginIsolated || !isBrowser)` rather than replicating the predicate.

title: detectTier() returns 'scalar' for both 'no WebAssembly' and 'WASM but no SIMD', undocumented in return type
description: The JSDoc comment on line 84 acknowledges the ambiguity but the `Tier` type and `detectTier()` signature give no indication that `'scalar'` is a catch-all for multiple distinct failure modes. `getCapabilities().selectedWasmBuild` returns `'none'` when WebAssembly is absent, which IS the correct differentiator, but `detectTier()` is also a public export. Callers who use only `detectTier()` cannot distinguish 'no WASM at all' from 'WASM but scalar-only' without reading internal comments. The fix is to add a JSDoc `@returns` clause or narrow the type to make the caller pattern explicit.

title: No runtime schema validation: Capabilities object is never structurally verified before being returned
description: All boolean flags are set from local probes in the same function so there is little risk of stale data, but `libjxlVersion` is always `'unknown'`, `deviceMemory` can be `null`, and the `selectedWasmBuild` field depends on `detectTier()` being consistent with `wasmSimd`/`wasmRelaxedSimd` flags. There is no assertion or schema check (e.g. zod/typebox/manual) that validates the returned object matches the `Capabilities` interface at runtime. Adding a single cheap structural validation before return would catch any future field omission or drift between the interface and the builder.

title: No test verifies that all fields of the Capabilities interface are present and typed correctly in the returned object
description: The tier-matrix and tier tests validate `selectedWasmBuild`, `wasm`, `wasmSimd`, and `wasmRelaxedSimd`, but fields like `webgpu`, `webnn`, `hardwareConcurrency`, `deviceMemory`, `imageDecoder`, `wasmExceptions`, `imageBitmap`, `offscreenCanvas`, and `libjxlVersion` are never asserted in any test case. If a future refactor accidentally drops or renames one of these fields, TypeScript compilation would catch it only if the type is consumed downstream — there is no runtime assertion that the full contract is fulfilled.
